tool.name: getITD
tool.description: detect FLT3-ITDs from amplicon NGS data
tool.command: getitd -nkern \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} #if $reference_source.reference_source_selector == 'history' -reference '$reference_source.reference' -anno '$reference_source.anno' #else -reference '$__tool_directory__/anno/amplicon.txt' -anno '$__tool_directory__/anno/amplicon_kayser.tsv' #end if -technology '$technology' -infer_sense_from_alignment '$infer_sense_from_alignment' -require_indel_free_primers '$require_indel_free_primers' #if $forward_primer -forward_primer \$(cat '$forward_primer') #end if #if $reverse_primer -reverse_primer \$(cat '$reverse_primer') #end if #if $forward_adapter -forward_adapter \$(cat '$forward_adapter') #end if #if $reverse_adapter -reverse_adapter \$(cat '$reverse_adapter') #end if -min_bqs $filtering.min_bqs -min_read_length $filtering.min_read_length -min_read_copies $filtering.min_read_copies -min_insert_seq_length $filtering.min_insert_seq_length -filter_ins_unique_reads $filtering.filter_ins_unique_reads -filter_ins_total_reads $filtering.filter_ins_total_reads -filter_ins_vaf $filtering.filter_ins_vaf -minscore_inserts $alignment.minscore_inserts -minscore_alignments $alignment.minscore_alignments -gap_open $alignment.gap_open -gap_extend $alignment.gap_extend -match $alignment.match -mismatch $alignment.mismatch -max_trailing_bp $alignment.max_trailing_bp -plot_coverage '$plot_coverage' -- sample '$library.fastq1' #if $library.library_type == 'paired' '$library.fastq2' #end if

param.name: reference_source_selector
param.label: Amplicon reference and annotation
param.help: Use the built-in FLT3 reference (Kayser amplicon) or supply your own.

param.name: require_indel_free_primers
param.label: Require indel-free primers
param.help: Discard reads with indels in, or missing, the primer sequence. Turn off if primers/adapters were already trimmed (-require_indel_free_primers).

param.name: forward_primer
param.label: Forward primer file (optional)
param.help: One primer sequence per line. Leave empty to use the getITD default (-forward_primer).

param.name: reverse_primer
param.label: Reverse primer file (optional)
param.help: One primer sequence per line. Leave empty for the getITD default (-reverse_primer).

param.name: forward_adapter
param.label: Forward adapter file (optional)
param.help: One adapter sequence. Leave empty for the getITD default (-forward_adapter).

param.name: reverse_adapter
param.label: Reverse adapter file (optional)
param.help: One adapter sequence. Leave empty for the getITD default (-reverse_adapter).

param.name: filter_ins_unique_reads
param.label: Minimum unique reads supporting an insertion

param.name: filter_ins_total_reads
param.label: Minimum total reads supporting an insertion

param.name: minscore_inserts
param.label: Min alignment score fraction for ITD detection

param.name: minscore_alignments
param.label: Min alignment score fraction for reads

param.name: max_trailing_bp
param.label: Max trailing bp for trailing-ITD detection

tool.help:
**What it does**
getITD_ detects and characterises FLT3 internal tandem duplications (ITDs) from amplicon sequencing data for minimal residual disease monitoring in AML.
To process several samples at once, use Galaxy's collection / batch handling on the read inputs: getITD then runs once per sample.
**Inputs**
* **Reads** -- single-end or paired-end FASTQ (plain or gzipped). * **Reference / annotation** -- the built-in FLT3 amplicon (default) or your own. * **Primers and adapters** -- optional files (one sequence per line). Leave empty to use getITD's built-in defaults.
**Outputs**
config, stats, coverage, and the ITD / insertion tables.
.. _getITD: https://github.com/tjblaette/getitd
